# UC-17.1.45 · Cisco ISE PassiveID — WMI / AD Agent / Syslog Provider Health

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Availability, Integration &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*When a person logs onto a Windows computer at the office, ISE learns who's using which IP address by listening to the company directory. We watch this listening line — if it goes silent, ISE no longer knows who's who, and other security tools that depend on that info also stop working.*

---

## Description

Detects PassiveID provider failures (WMI, AD Agent, syslog, SPAN, Endpoint, REST). PassiveID is how ISE acquires user-to-IP mappings without 802.1X — critical for non-supplicant networks where the user identity comes from AD logon events.

## Value

PassiveID is the user-context backbone for many NGFW deployments — Cisco FTD/FMC, Cisco Secure Firewall, third-party firewalls all consume PassiveID-derived user-to-IP mappings via pxGrid or REST. When PassiveID providers fail, downstream firewall policies that gate by user identity revert to anonymous treatment.

## Implementation

Confirm *PassiveID* logging category at INFO. Deploy OpenAPI poller. Schedule every 5 min, search window `-15m@m to @m`, trigger on count > 0, throttle suppression on `provider_name` for 30 min.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Stop the AD Agent service on a Domain Controller. Within 5 min ISE emits 91020 (AD Agent connection lost). UC-17.1.45 fires.

### Negative scenario

Restart the AD Agent. ISE reconnects. Alert clears.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- ISE PassiveID configured with at least one provider.
- `passiveid_providers.csv` with `provider_name`, `provider_type`, `is_critical`, `domain`.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable *PassiveID* category at INFO.

### Step 2 — Create the search
Search in `spl` field. Schedule every 5 min.

### Step 3 — Validate
Provoke as above.

### Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard "ISE — PassiveID Health":
- Per-provider status tile.
- Mapping rate timechart.
- Top-N error sources.

Alerting: PagerDuty medium on provider DOWN.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting
- **WMI fails** — DC's WMI namespace permission for ISE service account. PowerShell `wmic` test.
- **AD Agent missing** — installer not deployed on DC; deploy or update.
- **REST provider failures** — endpoint URL/cert/auth issue; validate from PSN CLI.

## SPL

```spl
(index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (91000, 91001, 91002, 91010, 91020, 91030))
  OR (index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:passiveid_health")
| stats count latest(_time) as last_event count(eval(MessageCode=="91001")) as drops count(eval(MessageCode=="91002")) as errors latest(provider_status) as status by provider_type, provider_name
| where drops > 1 OR errors > 0 OR status=="DOWN"
| sort - errors - drops
```

## Visualization

(1) Single value: providers DOWN. (2) Per-provider mapping rate timechart. (3) Top-N providers with errors.

## Known False Positives

- **AD Agent restart during patch** — Brief drop+reconnect, suppress isolated events.
- **WMI permission glitch on a single DC** — DC patch or GPO change may break WMI for one DC; reconnect after re-grant.
- **Mapping rate drops at night** — PassiveID volume tracks user logon volume; off-hours legit drops aren't failures.

- [Cisco ISE — AD Agent Configuration](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/security/ise/3-4/admin_guide/b_ise_admin_3_4/m_passiveid.html)
